channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: What misleading Meta Llama 4 benchmark scores show enterprise leaders about evaluating AI performance claims
item.description: Benchmarks are critical when evaluating AI — they reveal how well models work, as well as their strengths and weaknesses, based on factors like reliability, accuracy, and versatility.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Revolutionizing Antimatter Research: Turning Smartphone Tech into a Gigapixel Detector
item.description: A novel approach to detecting antiproton annihilation using repurposed smartphone camera tech and gigapixel devices
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: New observations show the asteroid that won't hit Earth resembles a spinning hockey puck
item.description: The asteroid that once had a small chance of striking Earth and now might slam into the moon resembles a spinning hockey puck.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump administration disbands crypto regulations enforcement unit
item.description: The Trump administration disbanded the National Cryptocurrency Enforcement Team (NCET) according to a Monday memo issued by the Department of Justice (DOJ) outlining their efforts to remove regulatory framework for the growing digital finance sector.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: IRS acting commissioner is resigning over deal to send immigrants' tax data to ICE, AP sources say
item.description: The Internal Revenue Service’s acting commissioner is resigning over a deal to share immigrants’ tax data with Immigration and Customs Enforcement for the purpose of identifying and deporting people illegally in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Intel's former CEO puts money into a little-known hardware startup that wants to make Nvidia obsolete
item.description: UK-based startup Fractile wants to make Nvidia obsolete and is backed by NATO and Intel's former CEO.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Genetically Engineered Wolves: A Step Towards Reviving Extinct Species?
item.description: Colossal Biosciences' genetically engineered wolves, resembling extinct dire wolves, have sparked debate on the feasibility of reviving lost species.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA LARSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s AI policy shifts focus to ‘high impact’ use cases
item.description: The White House says a new federal AI policy rejects the Biden administration's “risk-averse” approach, but one former official sees it as a “smart evolution." The post Trump’s AI policy shifts focus to ‘high impact’ use cases first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Redbox goes up for auction following a wild bankruptcy saga
item.description: Redbox is getting ready for one final sale. The defunct DVD rental chain’s assets, and those of its corporate siblings Crackle and Chicken Soup for the Soul Entertainment, are being auctioned off in New York later this month, according to a court filing published Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: Janko Roettgers, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft workers say they've been fired after 50th anniversary protest over Israel contract
item.description: Microsoft has fired two employees who interrupted the company's 50th anniversary celebration to protest its work supplying artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military, according to a group representing the workers.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Appeals panel clears way for DOGE access to sensitive personal data at OPM, Education Department
item.description: A federal appeals panel on Monday paused an order curtailing the Department of Government Efficiency's (DOGE) access to troves of sensitive personal data from three federal agencies, reopening the floodgates for the cost-cutting advisory board. In a 2-1 decision, a panel of judges on the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, The Hill

item.title: Musk, Navarro tangle on tariffs
item.description: Tech billionaire and close Trump ally Elon Musk tangled with the president's trade adviser Peter Navarro on tariffs over the weekend, as the administration’s new import taxes have roiled the stock market.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: 9 steps to take to prepare for a quantum future
item.description: Over the past year, vendor after vendor has reached the critical quantum-computing milestone where adding more qubits no longer adds a disproportionately higher amount of errors.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Why iPhone-as-a-service may make sense as tariffs bite Apple
item.description: It might be time for Apple-as-a-service as the company looks to plot a cunning course through the post-globalist freeze — and offering its products as a service could help it do just that. Apple had been expected to introduce just such a service.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google NotebookLM cheat sheet: Get fast insights into your docs
item.description: When Google first launched the NotebookLM beta in late 2023, the company billed it as an experimental note-taking app with a generative AI assistant built in. You can still use it to create notes, but the app has evolved into something much more novel and powerful.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 10 Java-based tools and frameworks for generative AI
item.description: Java is not the first language most programmers think of when they start projects involving artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML). Many turn first to Python because of the large number of Python-based frameworks and tools for AI, ML, and data science.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: GOP rep says Russia and China are 'laughing at us' after NSA director firing
item.description: Rep. Don Bacon (R-Neb.), a moderate Republican, slammed President Trump’s decision to fire the director of the National Security Agency (NSA) and his top deputy after demands from far-right influencer Laura Loomer, saying Sunday that China and Russia are “laughing at us” after that decision.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Congress pushes back at White House over Signal group chat
item.description: The White House’s assertion that the case is closed surrounding national security officials’ use of Signal is getting pushback in Congress, even among some Republicans who say their own probes will press on.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Apple just made it much easier to get a handle on your inbox. Here’s how
item.description: Keeping our inboxes organized often feels like an overwhelming task. If you’re fortunate, yours contains only messages from people you wish to communicate with. Realistically, though, most are cluttered with newsletters, receipts, social media digests, and more.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft employees protest at 50th anniversary party over Israel contract
item.description: A pro-Palestinian protest by Microsoft employees interrupted the company’s 50th anniversary celebration Friday, the latest backlash over the tech industry’s work to supply artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: GitHub upgrades tooling to help developers stop leaking secrets
item.description: Over 39 million API keys, credentials, and other secrets leaked onto GitHub’s platform last year, but an update to its scanning tool could help stop that.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: New Relic simplifies Kubernetes performance monitoring
item.description: New Relic this week announced a new capability in its Intelligent Observability Platform that the company says will enable engineering teams to deploy an eBPF-powered agent to automatically discover and monitor all applications running on Kubernetes clusters.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: A behind-the-scenes look at Nintendo Switch 2
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Nintendo's Switch 2 is bigger and better than its predecessor of eight years past as the video game developer pushes to bring its brand into “a new era" where social experiences are at the forefront.
item.dc:creator: RYAN KRYSKA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: New MLCommons benchmarks to test AI infrastructure performance
item.description: Industry consortium MLCommons has released new versions of its MLPerf Inference benchmarks, offering a closer look at how current-generation data center and edge hardware performs under increasingly demanding AI workloads.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta is fined by Turkey after refusing to restrict content on Facebook and Instagram
item.description: Meta said it has been hit with a hefty fine for resisting Turkish government demands to limit content on Facebook and Instagram.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: United receives FAA certification on Starlink-equipped aircraft
item.description: United has received FAA approval for a Starlink-equipped plane. The first commercial flight is set for May.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, NewsNation
